_meta.notes: Book I, Chapter 3 — Salt at the Threshold. The salt road is busier than it should be; merchants come down from the cathedral with their hair tied wrong.

title: Salt at the Threshold
theme: the salt road moves; small caravans go where caravans don't usually go
worldEvent: merchants from Salt Cathedral arrive in unexpected places, hair tied with the wrong knots
narratorPromptFragment: The current world-chapter is BOOK I, CHAPTER 3: SALT AT THE THRESHOLD. Salt is on the air everywhere — even where there's no reason for it. Merchants from the Salt Cathedral are appearing in towns that aren't on the trade routes; their hair is tied with wrong knots, their carts heavier than they should be. The Choristers are sending out a quiet word. Don't name the word.
